<!-- Generated by spec-lite | agent: plan_feature | date: 2026-05-30 -->

# Feature: Dynamic Function Variables (`_fn_` placeholders)

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-FP-DYNVAR-001
**Source**: Plan Feature agent (self-contained — no plan file)

Today, `{{placeholder}}` substitution in requests resolves **only** against user-defined
environment variables. Users have no built-in way to inject common dynamic values — a UUID,
a random number, today's date, a fake name/email/address — without manually maintaining them
as env vars.

This feature introduces a lightweight, **isolated, extensible** family of *dynamic function
variables* that reuse the existing `{{...}}` syntax but are namespaced with a reserved `_fn_`
prefix (e.g. `{{_fn_random_uuid}}`, `{{_fn_random_number(min=1, max=100)}}`). Functions are
evaluated at request-build time, anywhere `{{...}}` already works (URL, headers, params, body),
across the standard request path and flows.

The design optimizes for three things, in priority order:

1. **Simplicity & intuitiveness** — a self-documenting `name(key=value, ...)` syntax; no new
   mini-language.
2. **Extensibility/pluggability** — a registry + per-function arg schema so future
   user-configured functions and additional locales slot in without touching the engine.
3. **Documentation** — a dedicated README with copy-pasteable examples for every function.

## 2. Technical Context

> Standing rules (TypeScript strict, Vitest, JSDoc, kebab-case files, `packages/core` is
> platform-agnostic, etc.) live in `.spec-lite/memory.md` and are **not** repeated here.
> Only additions/overrides for this feature are listed.

### Approach

- **Reserved prefix.** A placeholder whose trimmed name starts with `_fn_` (case-insensitive)
  is a *function*, never an env-var lookup. `_fn_` is reserved — an env var literally named
  `_fn_*` is ignored and this is documented.

- **Single integration point.** [resolveParameterizedValue](packages/core/src/utils/common.ts#L489)
  is the universal chokepoint for `{{...}}` (URL, headers, params, body all flow through it).
  It gets exactly one new branch: if the matched placeholder is a function placeholder, delegate
  to the function engine; otherwise behave exactly as today. No call sites change.

- **Fresh value per occurrence.** Each `{{_fn_...}}` match is evaluated independently — two
  `_fn_random_uuid` placeholders yield two different UUIDs. No caching, simplest mental model.

- **Two public entry points** from the function module (`packages/core/src/utils/functions/index.ts`):
  - `resolveFunctionPlaceholder(innerText): { resolved: string } | null` — evaluates a single
    parsed placeholder; returns `null` when the placeholder is unknown/malformed so the caller
    can treat it as **unresolved** (mirrors current env-var behavior → blocks the request).
  - `validateFunctionTemplate(text): FnValidationError[]` — scans an entire string, parses every
    `_fn_` placeholder against its declared arg schema, and returns structured errors
    (unknown function, unknown arg, invalid value). The **UI** calls this for live validation;
    `buildHttpRequest` may also call it to surface precise errors before sending.
  - `isFunctionPlaceholder(innerText): boolean` — cheap `_fn_` prefix check used by the resolver.

- **Registry + arg schema = the pluggability seam.** Every function is an `FnDefinition` with a
  declared `args: FnArgSpec[]` (name, required, default, validate, description) and a `generate`
  function. The parser, the validator, and the README examples all derive from this one source.
  MVP registers only built-ins via `registerFunction()`; future user-defined functions register
  identically.

- **Data separated from code, locale-ready.** All dictionaries (names, cities, states, etc.) live
  in `packages/core/src/data/fnData.ts`, keyed by locale (`enUS`) behind a
  `getLocaleData(locale = 'en-US')` accessor. Generators never hard-code data. English/US only for
  MVP; other locales slot in without generator changes.

- **Zero new dependencies.** Date/time token formatting and randomness are implemented in-module
  (no `dayjs`/`faker`). `crypto.randomUUID()` is available in both Node 22 and modern browsers; a
  small fallback covers any gap.

### Syntax

```
{{_fn_<name>}}                                  # no customization
{{_fn_<name>(key=value, key=value, ...)}}       # named-arg customization
```

- Args are comma-separated `key=value` pairs; whitespace around tokens is trimmed.
- **Value constraint (documented):** arg values may not contain the parser delimiters
  `,` `(` `)` `}`. This covers every MVP function (charsets use concatenated ranges with no
  commas, e.g. `A-Za-z0-9`; override-lists use `|` separators). Quoting/escaping is a future
  extension.

### Spec-Specific Overrides

No overrides — see memory. (The module is platform-agnostic core code, uses `Result`-free simple
return shapes only for the internal parse path; all public fallible UI-facing surfaces return the
structured `FnValidationError[]` described above.)

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

N/A — no persisted/relational data model changes. The only "data" is the static in-memory
dictionary file below (not a DB schema).

### Static dictionary shape (`packages/core/src/data/fnData.ts`)

- **`LocaleData`** (interface):
  - `firstNames` (`string[]`), `lastNames` (`string[]`), `orgNames` (`string[]`)
  - `namePrefixes` (`string[]`) — Mr, Mrs, Ms, Dr, …
  - `nameSuffixes` (`string[]`) — Jr, Sr, II, III, PhD, …
  - `streetNames` (`string[]`), `streetTypes` (`string[]`) — St, Ave, Blvd, …
  - `secondaryUnitDesignators` (`string[]`) — Apt, Suite, Unit, … (for address line 2)
  - `cities` (`string[]`), `counties` (`string[]`)
  - `states` (`Array<{ full: string; abbr: string }>`)
  - `countries` (`Array<{ full: string; abbr: string }>`)
  - `emailDomains` (`string[]`) — example.com, test.com, …
- **`getLocaleData(locale?: string): LocaleData`** — returns `enUS` for `'en-US'` (default);
  designed to map additional locales later. Each list holds ~40–100 representative entries.

## 4. Files

**New — function engine (`packages/core/src/utils/functions/`):**

- `index.ts` — public API: `resolveFunctionPlaceholder`, `validateFunctionTemplate`,
  `isFunctionPlaceholder`; registers all built-ins.
- `types.ts` — `FnArgSpec`, `FnDefinition`, `FnParseResult`, `FnValidationError`.
- `parser.ts` — parses `_fn_name(key=value, ...)` into `{ name, args, errors }`.
- `registry.ts` — `registerFunction()`, `getFunction()`, `getAllFunctions()`.
- `formatters.ts` — `applyNumberFormat` (decimals/prefix/suffix), `formatDateTime` (token replacer),
  `pickRandom`, `randomInt`, `fillDigitPattern` (`#` → random digit), charset expansion.
- `generators/generic.ts` — `random_uuid`, `random_number`, `random_string`.
- `generators/datetime.ts` — `current_date`, `current_time`, `random_date`, `random_time`.
- `generators/person.ts` — `random_name`, `random_name_prefix`, `random_name_suffix`.
- `generators/address.ts` — `random_address`, `random_address_l1/_l2/_city/_state/_county/_country/_zip`.
- `generators/contact.ts` — `random_email`, `random_phone`, `random_ssn`.
- `README.md` — full feature documentation with examples (the dedicated README).

**New — data:**

- `packages/core/src/data/fnData.ts` — locale-keyed dictionaries (`enUS`) + `getLocaleData`.

**Modified:**

- `packages/core/src/utils/common.ts` — `resolveParameterizedValue` gains the `_fn_` branch.
- `packages/core/src/utils/flowResolver.ts` — ensure `_fn_` placeholders are passed through the
  flow resolution passes (not flagged as unresolved mid-flow) so they resolve at build time.
- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — export the new public API
  (`resolveFunctionPlaceholder`, `validateFunctionTemplate`, `isFunctionPlaceholder`, relevant types).

**New — tests (mirroring source under `packages/core/src/test/`):**

- `test/utils/functions/parser.test.ts`
- `test/utils/functions/registry.test.ts`
- `test/utils/functions/validateFunctionTemplate.test.ts`
- `test/utils/functions/generic.test.ts`
- `test/utils/functions/datetime.test.ts`
- `test/utils/functions/person.test.ts`
- `test/utils/functions/address.test.ts`
- `test/utils/functions/contact.test.ts`
- `test/utils/common.fnIntegration.test.ts` — `resolveParameterizedValue` `_fn_` routing + unresolved.
- `test/utils/flowResolver.fnPassthrough.test.ts` — flow pass-through.

## 5. Dependencies

- **None** new. Uses existing `crypto.randomUUID()` (Node 22 / browser) with an in-module fallback.
- Builds on the existing `{{...}}` resolution path; no other feature must ship first.

## 6. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Function engine end-to-end (parser, registry, validator, integration) + `random_uuid`

The full pipeline proven by one working function and the bad-input path.

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Create `types.ts` (`FnArgSpec`, `FnDefinition`, `FnParseResult`, `FnValidationError`),
    `registry.ts` (`registerFunction`/`getFunction`/`getAllFunctions`), `parser.ts`
    (parse `_fn_name(key=value, ...)`; detect unknown arg keys, malformed arg lists, value
    constraint violations), and `formatters.ts` helpers needed now (`pickRandom`, `randomInt`).
  - Create `index.ts` exposing `isFunctionPlaceholder`, `resolveFunctionPlaceholder`,
    `validateFunctionTemplate`; register `random_uuid` (no args; `crypto.randomUUID()` + fallback).
  - Modify `resolveParameterizedValue` in `common.ts`: when `isFunctionPlaceholder(trimmedName)`,
    call `resolveFunctionPlaceholder`; on `null` push to `unresolved` and keep the literal match
    (current behavior); otherwise substitute the resolved value. Non-`_fn_` names unchanged.
  - Modify `flowResolver.ts` so `_fn_` placeholders are left intact through both flow passes
    (resolved later at build time), never added to flow "unresolved".
  - Export the public API + types from `packages/core/src/index.ts`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**:
  - `parser.test.ts`: parses bare `_fn_random_uuid`; parses args; flags unknown function,
    unknown arg key, malformed `()`, and delimiter-in-value violations.
  - `registry.test.ts`: register/get/duplicate-name behavior; `getAllFunctions` returns defs.
  - `validateFunctionTemplate.test.ts`: clean template → `[]`; mixed valid + `{{_fn_typo}}` →
    one structured error; non-`_fn_` placeholders ignored.
  - `generic.test.ts` (uuid slice): `random_uuid` returns a valid v4 UUID; two placeholders differ.
  - `common.fnIntegration.test.ts`: `{{_fn_random_uuid}}` in a string resolves; `{{_fn_typo}}`
    lands in `unresolved`; env vars still resolve alongside functions.
  - `flowResolver.fnPassthrough.test.ts`: `_fn_` placeholder survives flow passes, not unresolved.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Create `functions/README.md` skeleton — overview, `_fn_` reserved
  prefix, named-arg syntax, value constraint, fresh-per-occurrence semantics, validation API,
  and the `random_uuid` entry. JSDoc on all public functions/types.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test` passes the TASK-001 suites; a manual
  `resolveParameterizedValue('id={{_fn_random_uuid}}', new Map())` yields a UUID with no unresolved,
  and `'{{_fn_typo}}'` reports unresolved.

### TASK-002: Numeric & string generics — `random_number`, `random_string`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `generators/generic.ts` add:
  - `random_number(min=0, max=100, decimals=0, prefix='', suffix='')` — integer when
    `decimals=0`, else `toFixed(decimals)`; wrap with prefix/suffix. Validate numeric min/max
    (min ≤ max) and non-negative integer decimals via `FnArgSpec.validate`.
  - `random_string(length=16, charset='A-Za-z0-9')` — expand range tokens (`A-Z`, `a-z`, `0-9`)
    plus literal chars into a pool; pick `length` chars. Validate positive integer length and
    non-empty expanded charset.
  - Add `applyNumberFormat` and charset-expansion helpers to `formatters.ts`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `generic.test.ts` — number within `[min,max]`; `decimals`/`prefix`/`suffix`
  formatting (`$12.34`); `min>max` → validation error; string length + charset membership;
  custom charset honored; invalid length/charset → validation error.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: README entries for `random_number` and `random_string` with the
  `(min=1, max=100, decimals=2, prefix=$, suffix=%)` and `(length=10, charset=A-Z0-9)` examples.
- **Verify**: targeted tests pass; README examples evaluate to plausible values.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-003: Date/time generics — `current_date`, `current_time`, `random_date`, `random_time`

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add `formatDateTime(date, format)` to `formatters.ts` supporting tokens
  `YYYY YY MM M DD D HH H mm m ss s` (and `MMM` month abbr). In `generators/datetime.ts`:
  - `current_date(format='YYYY-MM-DD')`, `current_time(format='HH:mm:ss')`.
  - `random_date(min, max, format='YYYY-MM-DD')` — random date in `[min,max]` (ISO inputs;
    sensible defaults, e.g. ~50 years back → today); validate parseable min/max and min ≤ max.
  - `random_time(min='00:00:00', max='23:59:59', format='HH:mm:ss')`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `datetime.test.ts` — token formatting correctness (deterministic via fixed
  date); `current_date`/`current_time` match expected pattern; `random_date` within range;
  bad/`min>max` ranges → validation error.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: README entries with a token-reference table and examples
  (`{{_fn_current_date(format=YYYY-MM-DD)}}`, `{{_fn_random_date(min=2020-01-01, max=2020-12-31)}}`).
- **Verify**: targeted tests pass; token table in README matches implemented tokens.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-004: Data layer + person names — `fnData.ts`, `random_name`, `random_name_prefix`, `random_name_suffix`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Create `packages/core/src/data/fnData.ts` with the `enUS` `LocaleData` and `getLocaleData`.
  - In `generators/person.ts`:
    - `random_name(type=person, parts=2)` — `person`: pick `parts` tokens from first/last name
      pools (1 = first only, 2 = first+last, 3+ = add middle names); `org`: pick an org name.
    - `random_name_prefix(values='')` — pick from `namePrefixes`, or from a user `|`-separated
      `values` override when provided.
    - `random_name_suffix(values='')` — same pattern over `nameSuffixes`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `person.test.ts` — person name has `parts` words from the dictionaries;
  `type=org` returns an org-pool value; `parts` bounds; prefix/suffix come from default pool and
  from a `values=` override; invalid `parts`/`type` → validation error.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: README entries for the three person functions, noting en-US data
  source and the `values=` override format; note the data file is the future localization seam.
- **Verify**: targeted tests pass; generated names are members of `fnData` pools.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-005: Address — `random_address` + 7 field variants

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `generators/address.ts`, using `getLocaleData`:
  - `random_address_l1` — `<number> <streetName> <streetType>`.
  - `random_address_l2` — `<designator> <number>` (e.g. `Apt 4B`).
  - `random_address_city`, `random_address_county`, `random_address_zip` (5-digit).
  - `random_address_state(format=abbr)` / `random_address_country(format=abbr)` — `full|abbr`.
  - `random_address` — composed single line `l1, city, STATE zip, COUNTRY` (no l2 by default).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `address.test.ts` — each variant's shape (regex for l1, 5-digit zip,
  state `full` vs `abbr`); composed `random_address` contains a city and a zip; invalid `format`
  → validation error.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: README section listing all address functions with examples and the
  `format=full|abbr` option for state/country.
- **Verify**: targeted tests pass; composed address reads as a plausible US address.
- **Depends on**: TASK-004 (shares `fnData.ts`)

### TASK-006: Contact — `random_email`, `random_phone`, `random_ssn`

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add `fillDigitPattern('#' → digit)` to `formatters.ts`. In
  `generators/contact.ts`:
  - `random_email(domain='')` — random local part (lowercased name-ish + digits); domain from
    `domain=` override or a random `emailDomains` entry.
  - `random_phone(format='(###) ###-####')` — fill `#` with digits; non-`#` chars kept literally.
  - `random_ssn(format='###-##-####')` — same fill semantics.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `contact.test.ts` — email matches a basic email regex and honors `domain=`;
  phone/ssn match the default formats; custom `format` (e.g. `+1-###-###-####`) honored;
  digit-count preserved.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: README entries for the three contact functions, documenting the
  `#`-digit format convention and the `domain=` override.
- **Verify**: targeted tests pass; outputs match the documented format patterns.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001 (and `formatters.ts` from TASK-002)

- **Verify**: README lists all built-in functions; the drift check passes;
  `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test` is green overall.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002, TASK-003, TASK-004, TASK-005, TASK-006

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A — functions resolve into request strings before transport; no auth interaction.
- **Error Handling**:
  - *Execution time* — unknown function or malformed args resolve to `null` and are added to the
    existing `unresolved` set, so `buildHttpRequest` blocks the request with its current
    "Unresolved placeholders" error (no new error channel, consistent UX).
  - *Authoring time (UI)* — `validateFunctionTemplate` returns structured `FnValidationError[]`
    (function name, arg, message) for inline display before sending.
  - Generators must be **total**: given schema-valid args they never throw; all rejection happens in
    the parser/validator against the arg schema.
- **Logging**: N/A — pure, synchronous string generation in `packages/core`; no I/O, no secrets.
  Do not log generated values.
- **Reserved-prefix collision**: `_fn_` is reserved; an env var named `_fn_*` is shadowed by the
  function engine. Documented in the README.
- **Flow interaction**: `_fn_` placeholders pass through flow resolution untouched and resolve at
  `buildHttpRequest` time, so functions work identically in single requests and flow nodes.
